text: Accurate citywide traffic flow prediction is fundamental to the advancement of intelligent transportation systems. Despite significant progress in modeling complex spatio-temporal correlations, existing methods frequently overlook two critical challenges: spatial heterogeneity, where diverse urban regions exhibit vastly different flow distributions, and temporal heterogeneity, where traffic patterns shift dynamically over time. Traditional models typically rely on shared parameter spaces that fail to account for these localized and time-varying nuances. To address these limitations, we introduce the Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework. ST-SSL integrates spatial and temporal convolutions to encode multi-dimensional information, enhanced by a novel self-supervised learning paradigm designed to capture heterogeneity. Specifically, our approach employs adaptive data augmentation at both the attribute and structural levels of the traffic graph. By constructing two auxiliary SSL tasks atop this augmented data, the framework sharpens its representation of idiosyncratic spatial and temporal patterns. Empirical evaluations across four benchmark datasets demonstrate that ST-SSL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Given the prevalence of spatio-temporal heterogeneity in real-world data, this framework offers a robust solution for a wide range of urban computing applications. Our implementation is publicly available at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
